selected_title: Leveraging firm innovation performance through alliance portfolios in emerging economies: the role of absorptive capacity
question: How does the evidence in “Leveraging firm innovation performance through alliance portfolios in emerging economies: the role of absorptive capacity” constrain claims about transfer from individual to organizational learning?

evidence_spans.text: Firms are increasingly engaging in alliance portfolios/networks -AP/Nets -to leverage their innovation performance -IP -and thus boost their competitiveness. Most studies, with a few exceptions especially from emerging countries, found a positive relationship between AP/Net and IP, depending on portfolio characteristics and firm absorptive capacity -AC. This article undertakes an empirical investigation of the AP/Net -IP relationship and ACs potentially moderating role in this relationship in an emerging country: Brazil. Statistical testing of hypotheses regarding these relationships, and ACs role, corroborated the exceptions. However, it confirmed how important it is that firms in an emerging country ensure a high level of AC to mitigate the negative effects of AP/Net diversity on IP. Some managerial and policy implications were also formulated.

learned_claims: The inspected source addresses Absorptive capacity, Business through the evidence summarized in the preserved span.
The abstract supplies a direct evidence span of 120 words; interpretation remains bounded to that span.
